pageDescription: Calculate your estimated 1RM with the Epley formula, compare against bodyweight strength standards, and see if you are Beginner, Novice, Intermediate, Advanced, or Elite.
importantBody: For best 1RM estimates, use 3–8 reps with good form. The Epley formula is less accurate for 1–2 reps or 12+ reps. Strength levels use bodyweight-relative standards adjusted for gender.
emptyHint: Enter your profile and lift data, then click “Calculate strength level” to see your 1RM and level.
proTip1: Test squat and deadlift when fresh; bench and overhead press after lower-body work if needed.
proTip2: Recalculate every 4–6 weeks to track real progress through training blocks.
proTip3: If one lift lags behind others, prioritize that movement in your program for balanced strength.
proTip4: Use the same unit (kg or lbs) for bodyweight and bar weight to avoid input errors.
proTip5: Pair this tool with a dedicated 1RM calculator when comparing multiple formulas.
errBodyWeight: Enter a valid body weight greater than zero.
errWeight: Enter a valid weight lifted greater than zero.
